reasoning: Deepseek's thorough audit found no malicious code in PKGBUILD or build-relevant source files. Qwen's 'inconclusive' was due to a disk quota error, not substantive findings. All reviewed files show standard build patterns without execution risks.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen's result was technical (disk quota), not analytical. Both models agreed on safety of reviewed files.
coverage_issues: console-0.1.8.tar.gz binary skipped - though non-executable during build, could theoretically hide payloads
false_positives: deepseek's '--dangerously-allow-all-builds' flag note - correctly identified as hygienic concern but not malicious
learnings: Improve disk quota handling to prevent technical failures
Prioritize PKGBUILD/.install files over dev configs (eslint.config.js was reviewed unnecessarily)
Add binary file analysis guidance - flag if executed during build, else lower priority
